title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games: Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: This article announces the Indian badminton squad for the Asian Games, highlighting the return of key players like Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: The Indian badminton squad for the upcoming Asian Games has been announced, featuring a strong lineup including PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, and the return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.

title: Is Tanvi Sharma's Game Developing More Like Saina Nehwal's Than PV Sindhu's?
indian_angle: The article provides an in-depth analysis of young Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma's playing style, comparing it to two of India's greatest badminton icons.
summary: An analytical piece delves into the evolving game of promising Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma, drawing comparisons between her style and those of former champions Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu, particularly after her recent performances.

title: Argentina for Chirag-Prannoy; Spain 'Maybe' for Ayush; Portugal/Germany for Sindhu
indian_angle: This light-hearted piece connects top Indian badminton players to the ongoing FIFA World Cup, offering a glimpse into their personal interests.
summary: A fun look at the FIFA World Cup preferences of India's badminton stars, speculating on which teams like Argentina, Spain, Portugal, or Germany might have the support of players such as Chirag Shetty, HS Prannoy, Ayush Shetty, and PV Sindhu.

title: How Doubles Coach Tan Kim Her Paired Up Hari-Arjun After Satwik-Chirag
indian_angle: The article provides insight into the strategic development of Indian men's doubles pairs by a key coach, following the success of Satwik-Chirag.
summary: An in-depth feature on how national doubles coach Tan Kim Her strategically formed the new men's doubles pair of Hariharan Amsakarunan and Arjun M.R., drawing on the successful blueprint established with Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty.

title: Why PV Sindhu Needs More Self-Belief to Beat An Se-young
indian_angle: This opinion piece critically examines what India's top women's singles player, PV Sindhu, needs to overcome a formidable rival.
summary: An opinion piece analyzing PV Sindhu's encounters with An Se-young, suggesting that a stronger sense of self-belief is crucial for the Indian ace to consistently defeat the dominant Korean shuttler, especially after their recent matches at the Indonesia Open.

title: No Respite for Chinese World No 15 Weng Hong Yang from Ayush Shetty
indian_angle: The report highlights a strong performance by young Indian talent Ayush Shetty against a higher-ranked international opponent.
summary: Young Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty delivered a challenging performance against Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang, pushing the experienced player and demonstrating his growing prowess on the international circuit.
